content.explanation:
**Wanted — Past Tense में चाहना:**

"Wanted" = चाहता था / चाहती थी / चाहते थे (past tense of want)

सभी subjects के साथ same form: I/He/She/We/They → **wanted**
(कोई he/she के लिए अलग form नहीं — unlike simple present where we add -s)

**Positive (Affirmative):**
Subject + **wanted** + to + base verb (desire to do an action)
Subject + **wanted** + noun (desire for a thing)

🇮🇳 मैं डॉक्टर बनना चाहता था।
🇬🇧 I **wanted** to become a doctor.

🇮🇳 उसे एक नई किताब चाहिए थी।
🇬🇧 She **wanted** a new book.

**Negative:**
Subject + **did not want** + to + base verb
Subject + **did not want** + noun

🇮🇳 मैं वहाँ नहीं जाना चाहता था।
🇬🇧 I **did not want** to go there.

**Question:**
**Did** + subject + **want** + to + verb?
**Did** + subject + **want** + noun?

🇮🇳 क्या तुम वहाँ जाना चाहते थे?
🇬🇧 **Did** you **want** to go there?

**Important:** Did के बाद हमेशा **want** (base form), never "wanted"!
rules: "Wanted" is the past tense of "want" — same for ALL subjects (I/He/She/We/They/It + wanted). No -s form needed.
Positive structure: Subject + wanted + to + base verb (e.g., I wanted to eat). OR Subject + wanted + noun (e.g., I wanted water).
Negative structure: Subject + did not want + to + base verb / noun. Note: 'did' already marks the past — do NOT say 'did not wanted'.
Question structure: Did + subject + want + to + verb? / Did + subject + want + noun? — 'Did' carries the past tense, so 'want' stays in base form.
"Wanted" can also mean 'needed/required' in formal/notice language: 'Wanted: Experienced software developer'.
Difference from 'want': 'want' = present desire (I want tea now); 'wanted' = past desire (I wanted tea earlier).
Wanted + to + verb vs Wanted + noun: Use 'to + verb' when desire is for an action (wanted to go); use just noun when desire is for a thing (wanted a car).
Do not confuse 'wanted to' with 'was wanting to' — English uses simple past (wanted), not past continuous for this desire expression.
Contracted negative: 'didn't want' = 'did not want' — both are correct; the contracted form is more common in spoken English.
In indirect speech, 'want' becomes 'wanted': She said she wanted to leave. (Not: She said she wants to leave, when reporting in past context.)
content.memoryTrick:
**"WANTED = Past WANT"** — बस want का past version है। सबके साथ same: wanted.
Did के बाद हमेशा want (base form) — याद रखो: Did ने already past बना दिया!

Trick: **D-W** rule — **D**id + **W**ant (base). Never 'Did + Wanted'.

examples.hindi: मैं interview में अच्छा perform करना चाहता था।
examples.english: I wanted to perform well in the interview.

examples.hindi: वे एक नया business शुरू करना चाहते थे।
examples.english: They wanted to start a new business.

examples.hindi: क्या प्रिया नई job ढूँढना चाहती थी?
examples.english: Did Priya want to find a new job?

examples.hindi: मैं उस समय कुछ नहीं बोलना चाहता था।
examples.english: I did not want to say anything at that time.

mistakes.why: 'Was wanting' (past continuous) is incorrect for expressing a past desire. Use simple past: 'wanted'.
'Wanted + that + subject + verb' is not standard English. Use: wanted + object + to + verb.
'Did' already carries the past tense. After 'Did', always use the base form: want (not wanted).
Spelling error: 'wanted' has only one 't'. Not 'wantted'.

mistakes.wrong: I did not wanted to go.
mistakes.correct: I did not want to go.
mistakes.why: After 'did not', use base form 'want', not 'wanted'.
After 'wanted', use 'to + base verb' (wanted to go), not 'wanted + verb+ing'.
'Does' is present tense. For past questions, use 'Did' + base verb (want).
'Wanted' is already past tense — no '-s' is added. It works the same for all subjects.
'Yesterday' signals past time, so the verb must also be past: 'wanted', not 'want'.
After 'didn't' (did not), the verb must be in base form: 'want' not 'wanted'.

speakingTips: अपनी childhood dreams बताओ: 'When I was young, I wanted to become a...' — यह phrase conversations में बहुत natural लगती है।
Past plans share करो जो change हो गए: 'I wanted to call you but I forgot.' / 'I wanted to attend but I was busy.'
Interview में: 'I wanted to join your company because...' — यह phrase आपकी genuine interest दर्शाता है।
Regret express करते हो: 'I wanted to do it sooner, but...' — यह honest और mature लगता है।
किसी के past desire के बारे में पूछना: 'Did you want to become something different when you were a child?' — great conversation starter!
Offer/request politely करना: 'I wanted to ask you something.' — 'want to' की जगह 'wanted to' use करने से tone softer और more polite लगती है।
Storytelling में: 'She wanted to leave, but she stayed.' — past desire + contrast creates engaging stories.
Business/professional: 'We wanted to discuss the proposal before proceeding.' — meetings और emails में common and professional.

mockTest.hindi: मेरे पिताजी नई ज़िम्मेदारी लेना चाहते थे।
mockTest.english: My father wanted to take on new responsibilities.
mockTest.explanation: "My father wanted to take on new responsibilities" — Past desire: subject + wanted + to + base verb.

essay.title: My Childhood Dreams
essay.hindi: इस essay में बचपन की इच्छाओं के बारे में बताया गया है। 'Wanted' का प्रयोग past desires और aspirations को express करने के लिए किया गया है।
essay.english:
When I was a child, I wanted to become an astronaut. I loved reading books about space and stars. My father always encouraged me. My sister wanted to become a doctor, and my brother wanted to become a cricketer. We all had big dreams.

As I grew up, things changed. I did not want to leave my city, so I chose a local college. My mother wanted me to study science, but I was more interested in computers. I wanted to learn programming, so I enrolled in a computer science course.

Did you ever want to become something different when you were young? Most people change their minds as they grow. What matters is that we try our best. I did not want to give up on my dreams. I worked hard, and today I am a software developer.

Looking back, I am glad I followed my passion. The skills I wanted to learn have made me successful. Every step I took brought me closer to the person I wanted to become.

essay.title: A New Job Opportunity
essay.hindi: इस essay में नई नौकरी के अवसर और उससे जुड़ी इच्छाओं का वर्णन है। 'Wanted' का प्रयोग professional goals को past tense में express करने के लिए किया गया है।
essay.english:
Last year, I wanted to find a new job. I was working at a small company, but I wanted to grow professionally. I did not want to stay in the same role for too long. I wanted to take on more responsibilities and earn a better salary.

I updated my resume and started applying. My colleague did not want to leave the company, but I was ready for a change. I wanted to join a team where I could learn and grow every day.

Did the new company want someone with my experience? I was nervous before the interview. I wanted to make a good impression. I prepared carefully and answered all the questions confidently.

After two rounds of interviews, I received an offer. I did not want to reject it. The company wanted me to start as soon as possible. I accepted the offer and joined the new team. It was the right decision, and I am very happy today.

essay.title: Learning English
essay.hindi: इस essay में English सीखने की journey के बारे में बताया गया है। 'Wanted' का प्रयोग language learning goals को express करने के लिए किया गया है।
essay.english:
I always wanted to speak English fluently. When I was in school, I did not want to speak in English because I was afraid of making mistakes. My classmates also did not want to look foolish in front of others. So we all stayed quiet.

But I knew that English was important for my career. I wanted to get a good job, and most companies wanted candidates who could communicate in English. I decided to change my approach.

I started practicing every day. I wanted to read English books, watch English movies, and speak with friends in English. Did my friends want to practice together? Yes, some of them did. We formed a small group.

Slowly, I improved. I did not want to stop learning. The more I practiced, the more confident I became. Today, I can speak English in meetings and interviews without fear. I wanted to be fluent, and I achieved that goal through consistent effort.

essay.title: A Family Decision
essay.hindi: इस essay में family के साथ मिलकर लिए गए एक ज़रूरी फ़ैसले का वर्णन है। 'Wanted' का प्रयोग different family members की इच्छाओं को express करने के लिए हुआ है।
essay.english:
Last year, our family wanted to move to a new city. My father wanted to be closer to his office. My mother wanted a bigger house with a garden. My sister wanted to join a better school. Everyone had different reasons, but we all agreed on one thing: we wanted a better life.

The decision was not easy. I did not want to leave my friends. My brother did not want to change his school either. We talked about it many times as a family.

Did we want to stay or go? In the end, we decided to move. My father found a new house, and we all helped to pack. My mother wanted everything to be well organized before we left.

After moving, we all adjusted quickly. The new city offered more opportunities. We did not want to regret the decision, and we did not. It was one of the best choices our family ever made together.

essay.title: Overcoming a Challenge at Work
essay.hindi: इस essay में एक office challenge को overcome करने की story है। 'Wanted' का प्रयोग past professional struggles और goals को express करने के लिए हुआ है।
essay.english:
Two years ago, I was given a very difficult project at work. My manager wanted me to complete it in just three weeks. I did not want to fail, but the timeline seemed very short. I wanted to ask for more time, but I also wanted to prove myself.

I made a plan. I wanted to divide the work into small tasks and finish each one on time. My team wanted to help, and we all worked together. We did not want to disappoint the client.

Did the client want any changes? Yes, they wanted a few modifications in the middle of the project. I did not want to panic. I stayed calm and managed the changes carefully.

In the end, we delivered the project on time. The client was happy, and my manager praised the team. I wanted to succeed, and we did. That experience taught me the value of planning, teamwork, and staying focused under pressure.

story.title: The Boy Who Wanted to Fly
story.english:
Once there was a young boy named Rohan. He always wanted to become a pilot. Every day, he watched aeroplanes in the sky and dreamed of flying. His parents wanted him to study medicine, but Rohan did not want to give up on his dream.

Rohan worked very hard in school. He wanted to pass all his exams with top marks. His teacher wanted him to focus on science and maths. Rohan listened carefully and studied every night.

When he was eighteen, he applied to a flying school. Did the school want good students? Yes, it did. Rohan passed the entrance test. He did not want to waste this chance. He trained hard for three years.

Finally, Rohan became a pilot. His parents were very proud. He wanted to show the world that dreams come true with hard work. Now he flies over the same city where he grew up, and he smiles every time.
story.hindi:
एक बार रोहन नाम का एक युवा लड़का था। वह हमेशा पायलट बनना चाहता था। हर दिन वह आसमान में हवाई जहाज़ देखता और उड़ने का सपना देखता था। उसके माता-पिता चाहते थे कि वह medicine पढ़े, लेकिन रोहन अपना सपना नहीं छोड़ना चाहता था।

रोहन ने स्कूल में बहुत मेहनत की। वह सभी परीक्षाओं में अच्छे नंबर लाना चाहता था। उसके अध्यापक चाहते थे कि वह science और maths पर ध्यान दे। रोहन ध्यान से सुनता और हर रात पढ़ता था।

जब वह अठारह साल का हुआ, उसने एक flying school में apply किया। क्या school अच्छे students चाहता था? हाँ, चाहता था। रोहन ने entrance test पास किया। वह यह मौका बर्बाद नहीं करना चाहता था। उसने तीन साल तक कड़ी मेहनत से training ली।

आखिरकार रोहन पायलट बन गया। उसके माता-पिता बहुत गर्व महसूस करते थे। वह दुनिया को दिखाना चाहता था कि मेहनत से सपने पूरे होते हैं। अब वह उसी शहर के ऊपर उड़ता है जहाँ वह पला-बढ़ा, और हर बार मुस्कुराता है।

story.title: The Perfect Interview
story.english:
Meera wanted to find a new job. She had been working at the same company for five years, and she wanted a better opportunity. She applied to a large technology firm.

The company called her for an interview. Meera did not want to be nervous. She prepared for two weeks. She wanted to know everything about the company. She practised her answers every night.

On the day of the interview, Meera arrived on time. The interviewer wanted to understand her experience and goals. Did Meera want the job badly? Yes, she did. She answered every question clearly and confidently.

At the end, the interviewer asked, 'Did you want to ask us anything?' Meera asked two smart questions. She wanted to know about the team and the growth opportunities.

A week later, Meera received the offer. She did not want to delay her response. She accepted immediately. It was the opportunity she had always wanted.
story.hindi:
मीरा नई नौकरी ढूँढना चाहती थी। वह पाँच साल से एक ही कंपनी में काम कर रही थी और एक बेहतर अवसर चाहती थी। उसने एक बड़ी technology कंपनी में apply किया।

कंपनी ने उसे interview के लिए बुलाया। मीरा घबराना नहीं चाहती थी। उसने दो हफ्ते तैयारी की। वह कंपनी के बारे में सब कुछ जानना चाहती थी। वह हर रात अपने जवाबों की practice करती थी।

Interview के दिन मीरा समय पर पहुँची। interviewer उसके अनुभव और लक्ष्यों को समझना चाहता था। क्या मीरा यह नौकरी बहुत ज़्यादा चाहती थी? हाँ, चाहती थी। उसने हर सवाल का जवाब साफ़ और आत्मविश्वास के साथ दिया।

अंत में interviewer ने पूछा, 'क्या आप हमसे कुछ पूछना चाहती थीं?' मीरा ने दो अच्छे सवाल पूछे। वह team और growth के अवसरों के बारे में जानना चाहती थी।

एक हफ्ते बाद मीरा को offer आया। वह अपना जवाब देर से देना नहीं चाहती थी। उसने तुरंत accept किया। यह वही अवसर था जो उसे हमेशा चाहिए था।

story.title: A Weekend with the Family
story.english:
Last weekend, our whole family wanted to spend time together. We did not want to sit at home. My father wanted to go to the hills. My mother wanted to visit a temple. My sister wanted to go shopping. I wanted to just relax and read a book.

We all discussed and decided to plan a day trip to a nearby hill station. Did everyone want to agree? My sister did not want to miss the shopping, so we planned to stop at a market on the way back.

We started early in the morning. The road was beautiful. My father drove the car, and my mother sang old songs. My sister and I laughed and talked the whole way.

At the hill station, we ate local food. The fresh air felt wonderful. My mother wanted to take many photos. We visited the temple, walked in the hills, and bought souvenirs at the market.

In the evening, we drove back home. We were all tired but very happy. It was exactly the day we all wanted.
story.hindi:
पिछले weekend हमारा पूरा परिवार एक साथ समय बिताना चाहता था। हम घर पर बैठना नहीं चाहते थे। मेरे पिताजी पहाड़ों पर जाना चाहते थे। मेरी माँ मंदिर जाना चाहती थीं। मेरी बहन shopping पर जाना चाहती थी। मैं बस आराम करना और किताब पढ़ना चाहता था।

हम सबने मिलकर बात की और एक पास के hill station की day trip plan की। क्या सब agree करना चाहते थे? मेरी बहन shopping miss नहीं करना चाहती थी, इसलिए हमने वापस आते वक्त एक market में रुकने का plan बनाया।

हम सुबह जल्दी निकले। रास्ता बहुत सुंदर था। मेरे पिताजी car चला रहे थे और माँ पुराने गाने गा रही थीं। मेरी बहन और मैं पूरे रास्ते हँसते-बोलते रहे।

Hill station पर हमने local खाना खाया। ताज़ी हवा बहुत अच्छी लगी। माँ बहुत सारी photos खींचना चाहती थीं। हमने मंदिर गए, पहाड़ों में घूमे और market से souvenirs खरीदे।

शाम को हम घर वापस आए। सब थके हुए थे लेकिन बहुत खुश थे। वह exactly वैसा दिन था जो हम सब चाहते थे।

dialogue.title: At the Office
dialogue.setting: At the office — Monday morning team meeting

turns.speaker: Manager
turns.hindi: सुबह की शुरुआत में, क्या तुम weekend पर काम करना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Good morning. Did you want to work over the weekend?

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: नहीं sir, मैं weekend पर आराम करना चाहता था। लेकिन मैंने कुछ emails check किए।
turns.english: No sir, I wanted to rest on the weekend. But I checked a few emails.

turns.speaker: Manager
turns.hindi: ठीक है। मैं तुम्हें एक नया project assign करना चाहता था।
turns.english: That's fine. I wanted to assign you a new project.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: बिल्कुल sir। क्या आप उस project के बारे में बताना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Of course sir. Did you want to tell me about the project?

turns.speaker: Manager
turns.hindi: हाँ। मैं इस project को इस हफ्ते शुरू करना चाहता था।
turns.english: Yes. I wanted to start this project this week.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: मैं इसमें पूरी तरह collaborate करना चाहता हूँ। team को कब involve करना चाहते थे?
turns.english: I wanted to fully collaborate on this. Did you want to involve the whole team?

turns.speaker: Manager
turns.hindi: हाँ, मैं पूरी team को involve करना चाहता था। Priya को भी बुलाओ।
turns.english: Yes, I wanted to involve the whole team. Call Priya too.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: ज़रूर। वह भी नए project पर काम करना चाहती थी।
turns.english: Sure. She also wanted to work on a new project.

turns.speaker: Manager
turns.hindi: बहुत अच्छा। मैं सभी को 10 बजे मिलना चाहता था।
turns.english: Very good. I wanted everyone to meet at 10 o'clock.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: मैं सबको inform करना चाहता था। अभी करता हूँ।
turns.english: I wanted to inform everyone. I will do it right now.

dialogue.title: Job Interview
dialogue.setting: A formal job interview at a company

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: नमस्ते, कृपया बैठिए। क्या आप हमारी company के बारे में कुछ जानना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Hello, please sit down. Did you want to know anything about our company?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: नमस्ते। हाँ, मैं company की work culture के बारे में जानना चाहता था।
turns.english: Hello. Yes, I wanted to know about the company's work culture.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: अच्छा। आप यह role क्यों लेना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Good. Why did you want to take this role?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: मैं अपनी skills develop करना चाहता था, और यह role उसके लिए perfect लगी।
turns.english: I wanted to develop my skills, and this role seemed perfect for that.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: आप अपनी पुरानी company क्यों छोड़ना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Why did you want to leave your previous company?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: मैं ज़्यादा ज़िम्मेदारी लेना चाहता था, जो वहाँ possible नहीं था।
turns.english: I wanted to take on more responsibility, which was not possible there.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: क्या आप हमें अपनी एक बड़ी उपलब्धि के बारे में बताना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Did you want to tell us about one of your biggest achievements?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: हाँ ज़रूर। मैं एक project में team lead करना चाहता था, और हमने deadline से पहले deliver किया।
turns.english: Yes, of course. I wanted to lead the team on a project, and we delivered before the deadline.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: Excellent। क्या आप हमसे कुछ पूछना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Excellent. Did you want to ask us anything?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: हाँ। मैं growth opportunities के बारे में जानना चाहता था।
turns.english: Yes. I wanted to know about the growth opportunities here.

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: बेटा, मैं तुमसे कुछ बात करना चाहती थी।
turns.english: Son, I wanted to talk to you about something.

turns.speaker: Son
turns.hindi: हाँ माँ, क्या आप कुछ कहना चाहती थीं?
turns.english: Yes Mum, did you want to say something?

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: तुम्हारे पिताजी और मैं तुम्हारी higher education के बारे में सोचना चाहते थे।
turns.english: Your father and I wanted to think about your higher education.

turns.speaker: Son
turns.hindi: माँ, मैं engineering करना चाहता था, लेकिन अब मेरा मन commerce में है।
turns.english: Mum, I wanted to do engineering, but now I am interested in commerce.

turns.speaker: Father
turns.hindi: बेटा, मैं तुम्हें force नहीं करना चाहता था। जो तुम चाहते हो, वो बताओ।
turns.english: Son, I did not want to force you. Tell us what you wanted.

turns.speaker: Son
turns.hindi: पापा, मैं MBA करना चाहता था और एक business शुरू करना चाहता था।
turns.english: Dad, I wanted to do an MBA and I wanted to start a business.

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: यह अच्छा idea है। क्या तुम पहले काम करना चाहते थे या सीधे पढ़ाई?
turns.english: That is a good idea. Did you want to work first or study directly?

turns.speaker: Son
turns.hindi: मैं पहले थोड़ा काम करना चाहता था ताकि practical experience मिले।
turns.english: I wanted to work for a while first to get practical experience.

turns.speaker: Father
turns.hindi: हम दोनों तुम्हारी मदद करना चाहते थे। हम support करेंगे।
turns.english: We both wanted to help you. We will support you.

turns.speaker: Son
turns.hindi: शुक्रिया पापा और माँ। मैं आप दोनों को proud करना चाहता था।
turns.english: Thank you Dad and Mum. I wanted to make you both proud.

dialogue.title: At a Restaurant
dialogue.setting: At a restaurant — friends ordering food

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: नमस्ते sir। क्या आप order करना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Hello sir. Did you want to order?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.hindi: हाँ, मैं कुछ vegetarian देखना चाहता था। क्या आपके पास कोई special dish है?
turns.english: Yes, I wanted to see something vegetarian. Do you have a special dish?

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: बिल्कुल sir। आज की special dal makhani और paneer butter masala है।
turns.english: Of course sir. Today's special is dal makhani and paneer butter masala.

turns.speaker: Sneha
turns.hindi: मैं paneer butter masala order करना चाहती थी, लेकिन मैं कुछ नया try करना चाहती हूँ।
turns.english: I wanted to order paneer butter masala, but I want to try something new.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: क्या आप मेरी recommendation सुनना चाहती थीं? हमारी mushroom curry बहुत popular है।
turns.english: Did you want to hear my recommendation? Our mushroom curry is very popular.

turns.speaker: Sneha
turns.hindi: हाँ, मैं वही try करना चाहती हूँ। एक mushroom curry please।
turns.english: Yes, I wanted to try that. One mushroom curry please.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.hindi: मुझे dal makhani चाहिए। और मैं garlic naan भी order करना चाहता था।
turns.english: I will have the dal makhani. And I wanted to order garlic naan too.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: ठीक है। क्या आप कुछ पीना चाहते थे?
turns.english: Alright. Did you want anything to drink?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.hindi: मैं मसाला chai चाहता था, और Sneha के लिए एक cold coffee।
turns.english: I wanted masala chai, and a cold coffee for Sneha.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: ज़रूर sir। आपका order कुछ देर में आ जाएगा।
